revision.comment: 新しいページ：高松茶臼山古墳（香川県高松市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 高松茶臼山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[香川県]][[高松市]]前田西町・新田町・東山崎町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 30.10 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 6|経度秒 = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m（または66m）
高さ2.4m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]2基 |出土品 = 鍬形石など副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 香川県指定史跡「高松市茶臼山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kagawa |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル = 高松茶臼山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{座標一覧}} '''高松茶臼山古墳'''（たかまつちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[香川県]][[高松市]]前田西町・新田町・東山崎町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。香川県指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「高松市茶臼山古墳」）。 == 概要 == 香川県中部、[[高松平野]]東部の、久米山から東に延びる東茶臼山丘陵上に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方向に向ける。墳丘は地山整形の上に盛土で構築されており{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}、墳丘長は75メートル{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または66メートル史跡説明板。）を測る。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか[[埴輪]]（壺形埴輪か）が認められる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。主体部の埋葬施設は後円部墳頂における[[竪穴式石室]]2基で、いずれも石室主軸を東西方向とする。発掘調査では、両石室から鍬形石を始めとする副葬品が検出されている。 この高松茶臼山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]末葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。前方後円墳・長大な竪穴式石室・[[割竹形木棺]]の使用（推定）・鍬形石の出土の点で畿内的様相の強い古墳であり{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}、高松平野西部における在地色の強い積石塚古墳群である[[石清尾山古墳群]]との間で、高松平野における政治的状況および畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関係性を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。 古墳域は[[1970年]]（昭和45年）に香川県指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）、発掘調査（香川県教育委員会・高松市教育委員会・香川県文化財保護協会）{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）8月8日、「高松市茶臼山古墳」として香川県指定史跡に指定。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 墳丘長：75メートル（または66メートル） * 後円部 ** 直径：35メートル ** 高さ：2.4メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：26メートル ** 高さ：1.8メートル File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|左に1号石室、右奥に2号石室。}}]] 主体部の埋葬施設としては後円部墳頂において2基の[[竪穴式石室]]が構築されている。いずれも石室主軸を東西方向とする。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 1号石室（第1主体部：南石室） *: 後円部墳頂南側、墳丘を掘り込んだ墓壙に川原石・粘土を敷いたのち、石室が構築される{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墓壙は東西（長軸）10.7メートル・南北（短軸）6.2メートルを、石室は長さ5.4メートル・最大幅1.1メートル・高さ1.35メートルを測る{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。石室の石材は[[安山岩]]の板石で、小口積みされる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。天井石の上面は粘土で被覆される。発掘調査では、石室内から人骨2体のほか鍬形石などの副葬品（後述）が検出されている。 * 2号石室（第2主体部：北石室） *: 石室は長さ5.9メートル・最大幅0.8メートル・高さ0.75メートルを測る{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。石室の石材は安山岩{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または[[花崗岩]]{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}）の小礫である。天井石の上面は粘土で被覆される。発掘調査では石室内から副葬品（後述）が検出されている。 多くの点で畿内色の強い高松茶臼山古墳であるが、石室主軸を東西方向とする点では在地色を示す。 そのほか、前方部で組合式箱形石棺2基、前方部端部裾で組合式箱形石棺2基・土壙墓2基が認められている{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|1号石室内部 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|2号石室内部 == 出土品 == 発掘調査で石室内から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 1号石室 ** 画文帯同行式神獣鏡 1 - 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。 ** 碧玉製鍬形石 2 - 人骨2体の間。朱が塗られる。 ** 管玉 ** 勾玉 ** ガラス製小玉 ** 大刀 1 ** 鉄剣 5 ** 鉄鏃 1 ** [[土師器]]壺 2 * 2号石室 ** 鉄鏃 2 ** 鏃形大型鉄器 2 ** 鉄剣 1 ** 鉄斧 1 ** 鉇 1 ** 小玉 2 鍬形石の出土は、香川県内では唯一の例になる。 なお、前方部の埋葬施設からは副葬品は検出されていない{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 == 文化財 == === 香川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 高松市茶臼山古墳 - 1970年（昭和45年）8月8日指定[http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html 高松市茶臼山古墳]（高松市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（高松市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 38 香川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=三宅良明|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=|title=高松市の文化財 -まもるまなぶつたえる-|publisher=高松市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=香川県埋蔵文化財センター編|author=|year=2014|chapter=|title=高松市茶臼山古墳|publisher=香川県教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[久本古墳|久本古墳・山下古墳]] - 高松茶臼山古墳の北に所在する後期古墳。久本古墳は高松市指定史跡。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html 高松市茶臼山古墳] - 高松市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:たかまつちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:香川県の古墳]] [[Category:高松市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:香川県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 高松茶臼山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[香川県]][[高松市]]前田西町・新田町・東山崎町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 30.10 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 6|経度秒 = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m（または66m）
高さ2.4m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]2基 |出土品 = 鍬形石など副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 香川県指定史跡「高松市茶臼山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kagawa |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル = 高松茶臼山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{座標一覧}} '''高松茶臼山古墳'''（たかまつちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[香川県]][[高松市]]前田西町・新田町・東山崎町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。香川県指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「高松市茶臼山古墳」）。 == 概要 == 香川県中部、[[高松平野]]東部の、久米山から東に延びる東茶臼山丘陵上に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方向に向ける。墳丘は地山整形の上に盛土で構築されており{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}、墳丘長は75メートル{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または66メートル史跡説明板。）を測る。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか[[埴輪]]（壺形埴輪か）が認められる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。主体部の埋葬施設は後円部墳頂における[[竪穴式石室]]2基で、いずれも石室主軸を東西方向とする。発掘調査では、両石室から鍬形石を始めとする副葬品が検出されている。 この高松茶臼山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]末葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。前方後円墳・長大な竪穴式石室・[[割竹形木棺]]の使用（推定）・鍬形石の出土の点で畿内的様相の強い古墳であり{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}、高松平野西部における在地色の強い積石塚古墳群である[[石清尾山古墳群]]との間で、高松平野における政治的状況および畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関係性を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。 古墳域は[[1970年]]（昭和45年）に香川県指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）、発掘調査（香川県教育委員会・高松市教育委員会・香川県文化財保護協会）{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）8月8日、「高松市茶臼山古墳」として香川県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 墳丘長：75メートル（または66メートル） * 後円部 ** 直径：35メートル ** 高さ：2.4メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：26メートル ** 高さ：1.8メートル File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|左に1号石室、右奥に2号石室。}}]] 主体部の埋葬施設としては後円部墳頂において2基の[[竪穴式石室]]が構築されている。いずれも石室主軸を東西方向とする。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 1号石室（第1主体部：南石室） *: 後円部墳頂南側、墳丘を掘り込んだ墓壙に川原石・粘土を敷いたのち、石室が構築される{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墓壙は東西（長軸）10.7メートル・南北（短軸）6.2メートルを、石室は長さ5.4メートル・最大幅1.1メートル・高さ1.35メートルを測る{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。石室の石材は[[安山岩]]の板石で、小口積みされる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。天井石の上面は粘土で被覆される。発掘調査では、石室内から人骨2体のほか鍬形石などの副葬品（後述）が検出されている。 * 2号石室（第2主体部：北石室） *: 石室は長さ5.9メートル・最大幅0.8メートル・高さ0.75メートルを測る{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。石室の石材は安山岩{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または[[花崗岩]]{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}）の小礫である。天井石の上面は粘土で被覆される。発掘調査では石室内から副葬品（後述）が検出されている。 多くの点で畿内色の強い高松茶臼山古墳であるが、石室主軸を東西方向とする点では在地色を示す。 そのほか、前方部で組合式箱形石棺2基、前方部端部裾で組合式箱形石棺2基・土壙墓2基が認められている{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|1号石室内部 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|2号石室内部 == 出土品 == 発掘調査で石室内から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 1号石室 ** 画文帯同行式神獣鏡 1 - 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。 ** 碧玉製鍬形石 2 - 人骨2体の間。朱が塗られる。 ** 管玉 ** 勾玉 ** ガラス製小玉 ** 大刀 1 ** 鉄剣 5 ** 鉄鏃 1 ** [[土師器]]壺 2 * 2号石室 ** 鉄鏃 2 ** 鏃形大型鉄器 2 ** 鉄剣 1 ** 鉄斧 1 ** 鉇 1 ** 小玉 2 鍬形石の出土は、香川県内では唯一の例になる。 なお、前方部の埋葬施設からは副葬品は検出されていない{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 == 文化財 == === 香川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 高松市茶臼山古墳 - 1970年（昭和45年）8月8日指定[http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html 高松市茶臼山古墳]（高松市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 高松茶臼山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[香川県]][[高松市]]前田西町・新田町・東山崎町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 30.10 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 6|経度秒 = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m（または66m）
高さ2.4m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]2基 |出土品 = 鍬形石など副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 香川県指定史跡「高松市茶臼山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kagawa |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル = 高松茶臼山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{座標一覧}} '''高松茶臼山古墳'''（たかまつちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[香川県]][[高松市]]前田西町・新田町・東山崎町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。香川県指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「高松市茶臼山古墳」）。 == 概要 == 香川県中部、[[高松平野]]東部の、久米山から東に延びる東茶臼山丘陵上に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方向に向ける。墳丘は地山整形の上に盛土で構築されており{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}、墳丘長は75メートル{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または66メートル史跡説明板。）を測る。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか[[埴輪]]（壺形埴輪か）が認められる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は後円部墳頂における[[竪穴式石室]]2基で、いずれも石室主軸を東西方向とする。発掘調査では、両石室から鍬形石を始めとする副葬品が検出されている。 この高松茶臼山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]末葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。前方後円墳・長大な竪穴式石室・[[割竹形木棺]]の使用（推定）・鍬形石の出土の点で畿内的様相の強い古墳であり{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}、高松平野西部における在地色の強い積石塚古墳群である[[石清尾山古墳群]]との間で、高松平野における政治的状況および畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関係性を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。 古墳域は[[1970年]]（昭和45年）に香川県指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）、発掘調査（香川県教育委員会・高松市教育委員会・香川県文化財保護協会）{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）8月8日、「高松市茶臼山古墳」として香川県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 墳丘長：75メートル（または66メートル） * 後円部 ** 直径：35メートル ** 高さ：2.4メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：26メートル ** 高さ：1.8メートル File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|左に1号石室、右奥に2号石室。}}]] 埋葬施設としては後円部墳頂において2基の[[竪穴式石室]]が構築されている。いずれも石室主軸を東西方向とする。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 1号石室（第1主体部：南石室） *: 後円部墳頂南側、墳丘を掘り込んだ墓壙に川原石・粘土を敷いたのち、石室が構築される{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墓壙は東西（長軸）10.7メートル・南北（短軸）6.2メートルを、石室は長さ5.4メートル・最大幅1.1メートル・高さ1.35メートルを測る{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。石室の石材は[[安山岩]]の板石で、小口積みされる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。天井石の上面は粘土で被覆される。発掘調査では、石室内から人骨2体のほか鍬形石などの副葬品（後述）が検出されている。 * 2号石室（第2主体部：北石室） *: 石室は長さ5.9メートル・最大幅0.8メートル・高さ0.75メートルを測る{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。石室の石材は安山岩{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または[[花崗岩]]{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}）の小礫である。天井石の上面は粘土で被覆される。発掘調査では石室内から副葬品（後述）が検出されている。 多くの点で畿内色の強い高松茶臼山古墳であるが、石室主軸を東西方向とする点では在地色を示す。 そのほか、前方部で組合式箱形石棺2基、前方部端部裾で組合式箱形石棺2基・土壙墓2基が認められている{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|1号石室内部 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|2号石室内部 == 出土品 == 発掘調査で石室内から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 1号石室 ** 画文帯同行式神獣鏡 1 - 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。 ** 碧玉製鍬形石 2 - 人骨2体の間。朱が塗られる。 ** 管玉 ** 勾玉 ** ガラス製小玉 ** 大刀 1 ** 鉄剣 5 ** 鉄鏃 1 ** [[土師器]]壺 2 * 2号石室 ** 鉄鏃 2 ** 鏃形大型鉄器 2 ** 鉄剣 1 ** 鉄斧 1 ** 鉇 1 ** 小玉 2 鍬形石の出土は、香川県内では唯一の例になる。 なお、前方部の埋葬施設からは副葬品は検出されていない{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 == 文化財 == === 香川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 高松市茶臼山古墳 - 1970年（昭和45年）8月8日指定[http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html 高松市茶臼山古墳]（高松市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（高松市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 38 香川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=三宅良明|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=|title=高松市の文化財 -まもるまなぶつたえる-|publisher=高松市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=香川県埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=2014|chapter=|title=高松市茶臼山古墳|publisher=香川県教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[久本古墳|久本古墳・山下古墳]] - 高松茶臼山古墳の北に所在する後期古墳。久本古墳は高松市指定史跡。 == 外部リンク == {{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |Image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Ishizuchi Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * **Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):** Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * **Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):** Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * **Stone Chamber 1**: - {{ill|Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) - Two magatama stones (with red pigment) - Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads - Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * **Stone Chamber 2**: - Two iron spearheads - One axe - Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * **Historic Site**: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]]

However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Ishizuchi Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * **Stone Chamber 1**: - {{ill|Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) - Two magatama stones (with red pigment) - Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads - Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * **Stone Chamber 2**: - Two iron spearheads - One axe - Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * **Historic Site**: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:New pages]][[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]]

The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * **Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):** Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * **Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):** Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * **Stone Chamber 1**: - {{ill|Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) - Two magatama stones (with red pigment) - Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads - Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * **Stone Chamber 2**: - Two iron spearheads - One axe - Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * **Historic Site**: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |Image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Ishizuchi Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * **Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):** Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * **Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):** Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * **Stone Chamber 1**: - {{ill|Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) - Two magatama stones (with red pigment) - Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads - Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * **Stone Chamber 2**: - Two iron spearheads - One axe - Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * **Historic Site**: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |Image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Ishizuchi Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * **Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):** Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * **Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):** Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * **Stone Chamber 1**: - {{ill|Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) - Two magatama stones (with red pigment) - Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads - Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * **Stone Chamber 2**: - Two iron spearheads - One axe - Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * **Historic Site**: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]]

Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Ishizuchi Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * **Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):** Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * **Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):** Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * **Stone Chamber 1**: - {{ill|Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) - Two magatama stones (with red pigment) - Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads - Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * **Stone Chamber 2**: - Two iron spearheads - One axe - Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * **Historic Site**: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Ishizuchi Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * **Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):** Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Ishizuchi Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Ishizuchi Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': - {{ill|Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) - Two magatama stones (with red pigment) - Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads - Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': - Two iron spearheads - One axe - Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |Image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': - {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) - Two magatama stones (with red pigment) - Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads - Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': - Two iron spearheads - One axe - Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - {{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |Image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': - {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) - Two magatama stones (with red pigment) - Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads - Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': - Two iron spearheads - One axe - Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |Image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun -
