{{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |Image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - {{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - {{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - {{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - {{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Japanese Kofun |Name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Alias = |Belonging = |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 18|Latitude seconds = 30.10 |Longitude degrees = 134|Longitude minutes = 6|Longitude seconds = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Scale = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |Burial facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (2) |Excavated items = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Kagawa |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |Label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Kofun |Label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric site information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole shape, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Dimensions: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' Dimensions: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}.
