Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinatess = 34|coordinatess = 18|coordinatess = 30.10 |coordinatess = 134|coordinatess = 6|coordinatess = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |other_designation = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersother_designation information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designated as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture other_designation Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == other_designation == === Kagawa Prefecture other_designation === * '''other_designation''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designated August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersother_designation information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture other_designation Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == other_designation == === Kagawa Prefecture other_designation === * '''other_designation''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designation1 August 8, 1970). == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * The site was designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture other_designation Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == Burial Facilities == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == other_designation == === Kagawa Prefecture other_designation === * '''other_designation''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designation1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersother_designation information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture other_designation Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == other_designation == === Kagawa Prefecture other_designation === * '''other_designation''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designation1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 18|coordinates = 30.10 |coordinates = 134|coordinates = 6|coordinates = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |other_designation = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersother_designation information board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture other_designation Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == other_designation == === Kagawa Prefecture other_designation === * '''other_designation''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (designation1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Information board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 18|coordinates = 30.10 |coordinates = 134|coordinates = 6|coordinates = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |other_other_designation = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been other_designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersother_other_designation intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was other_designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture other_other_designation Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == other_other_designation == === Kagawa Prefecture other_other_designation === * '''other_other_designation''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (other_designation1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 18|coordinates = 30.10 |coordinates = 134|coordinates = 6|coordinates = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |other_other_designation = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label position = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been other_designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersother_other_designation intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was other_designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture other_other_designation Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == other_other_designation == === Kagawa Prefecture other_other_designation === * '''other_other_designation''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (other_designation1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been other_designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersother_designation intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was other_designation1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture other_designation Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == other_designation == === Kagawa Prefecture other_designation === * '''other_designation''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (other_designation1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 18|coordinates = 30.10 |coordinates = 134|coordinates = 6|coordinates = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic Site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳}} == External Links == * revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|12=draft}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 18|coordinates = 30.10 |coordinates = 134|coordinates = 6|coordinates = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic Site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|12=draft}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡|12=draft}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:akamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]

Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|12=draft}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 18|coordinates = 30.10 |coordinates = 134|coordinates = 6|coordinates = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic Site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|12=draft}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡|12=draft}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun -
