{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 高松茶臼山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[香川県]][[高松市]]前田西町・新田町・東山崎町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 30.10 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 6|経度秒 = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m（または66m）
高さ2.4m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]2基 |出土品 = 鍬形石など副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 香川県指定史跡「高松市茶臼山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kagawa |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル = 高松茶臼山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{座標一覧}} '''高松茶臼山古墳'''（たかまつちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[香川県]][[高松市]]前田西町・新田町・東山崎町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。香川県指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「高松市茶臼山古墳」）。 == 概要 == 香川県中部、[[高松平野]]東部の、久米山から東に延びる東茶臼山丘陵上に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方向に向ける。墳丘は地山整形の上に盛土で構築されており{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}、墳丘長は75メートル{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または66メートル史跡説明板。）を測る。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか[[埴輪]]（壺形埴輪か）が認められる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は後円部墳頂における[[竪穴式石室]]2基で、いずれも石室主軸を東西方向とする。発掘調査では、両石室から鍬形石を始めとする副葬品が検出されている。 この高松茶臼山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]末葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。前方後円墳・長大な竪穴式石室・[[割竹形木棺]]の使用（推定）・鍬形石の出土の点で畿内的様相の強い古墳であり{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}、高松平野西部における在地色の強い積石塚古墳群である[[石清尾山古墳群]]との間で、高松平野における政治的状況および畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関係性を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。被葬者の[[Y染色体ハプログループ]]は縄文系のC1a1に判定された{{Cite web |url=https://rekihaku.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/2677/files/kenkyuhokoku_228_31.pdf |title=香川県高松市高松茶臼山古墳出土 古墳前期人骨の核DNA分析 |access-date=2025-04-28}}。 古墳域は[[1970年]]（昭和45年）に香川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）、発掘調査（香川県教育委員会・高松市教育委員会・香川県文化財保護協会）{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）8月8日、「高松市茶臼山古墳」として香川県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 墳丘長：75メートル（または66メートル） * 後円部 ** 直径：35メートル ** 高さ：2.4メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：26メートル ** 高さ：1.8メートル File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|左に1号石室、右奥に2号石室。}}]] 埋葬施設としては後円部墳頂において2基の[[竪穴式石室]]が構築されている。いずれも石室主軸を東西方向とする。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 1号石室（第1主体部：南石室） *: 後円部墳頂南側、墳丘を掘り込んだ墓壙に川原石・粘土を敷いたのち、石室が構築される{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墓壙は東西（長軸）10.7メートル・南北（短軸）6.2メートルを、石室は長さ5.4メートル・最大幅1.1メートル・高さ1.35メートルを測る{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。石室の石材は[[安山岩]]の板石で、小口積みされる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。天井石の上面は粘土で被覆される。発掘調査では、石室内から人骨2体のほか鍬形石などの副葬品（後述）が検出されている。 * 2号石室（第2主体部：北石室） *: 石室は長さ5.9メートル・最大幅0.8メートル・高さ0.75メートルを測る{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。石室の石材は安山岩{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または[[花崗岩]]{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}）の小礫である。天井石の上面は粘土で被覆される。発掘調査では石室内から副葬品（後述）が検出されている。 多くの点で畿内色の強い高松茶臼山古墳であるが、石室主軸を東西方向とする点では在地色を示す。 そのほか、前方部で組合式箱形石棺2基、前方部端部裾で組合式箱形石棺2基・土壙墓2基が認められている{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|1号石室内部 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|2号石室内部 == 出土品 == 発掘調査で石室内から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 1号石室 ** 画文帯同行式神獣鏡 1 - 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。 ** 碧玉製鍬形石 2 - 人骨2体の間。朱が塗られる。 ** 管玉 ** 勾玉 ** ガラス製小玉 ** 大刀 1 ** 鉄剣 5 ** 鉄鏃 1 ** [[土師器]]壺 2 * 2号石室 ** 鉄鏃 2 ** 鏃形大型鉄器 2 ** 鉄剣 1 ** 鉄斧 1 ** 鉇 1 ** 小玉 2 鍬形石の出土は、香川県内では唯一の例になる。 なお、前方部の埋葬施設からは副葬品は検出されていない{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 == 文化財 == === 香川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 高松市茶臼山古墳 - 1970年（昭和45年）8月8日指定[http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html 高松市茶臼山古墳]（高松市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（高松市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 38 香川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=三宅良明|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=|title=高松市の文化財 -まもるまなぶつたえる-|publisher=高松市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=香川県埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=2014|chapter=|title=高松市茶臼山古墳|publisher=香川県教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[久本古墳|久本古墳・山下古墳]] - 高松茶臼山古墳の北に所在する後期古墳。久本古墳は高松市指定史跡。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html 高松市茶臼山古墳] - 高松市ホームページ revision.comment: 7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:高松茶臼山古墳 up to rev 104653395
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 高松茶臼山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[香川県]][[高松市]]前田西町・新田町・東山崎町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 30.10 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 6|経度秒 = 15.52 |ISO = JP-37 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m（または66m）
高さ2.4m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]2基 |出土品 = 鍬形石など副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 香川県指定史跡「高松市茶臼山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kagawa |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル = 高松茶臼山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{座標一覧}} '''高松茶臼山古墳'''（たかまつちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[香川県]][[高松市]]前田西町・新田町・東山崎町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。香川県指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「高松市茶臼山古墳」）。 == 概要 == 香川県中部、[[高松平野]]東部の、久米山から東に延びる東茶臼山丘陵上に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方向に向ける。墳丘は地山整形の上に盛土で構築されており{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}、墳丘長は75メートル{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または66メートル史跡説明板。）を測る。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか[[埴輪]]（壺形埴輪か）が認められる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は後円部墳頂における[[竪穴式石室]]2基で、いずれも石室主軸を東西方向とする。発掘調査では、両石室から鍬形石を始めとする副葬品が検出されている。 この高松茶臼山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]末葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。前方後円墳・長大な竪穴式石室・[[割竹形木棺]]の使用（推定）・鍬形石の出土の点で畿内的様相の強い古墳であり{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}、高松平野西部における在地色の強い積石塚古墳群である[[石清尾山古墳群]]との間で、高松平野における政治的状況および畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関係性を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。被葬者の[[Y染色体ハプログループ]]は縄文系のC1a1に判定された{{Cite web |url=https://rekihaku.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/2677/files/kenkyuhokoku_228_31.pdf |title=香川県高松市高松茶臼山古墳出土 古墳前期人骨の核DNA分析 |access-date=2025-04-28}}。 古墳域は[[1970年]]（昭和45年）に香川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）、発掘調査（香川県教育委員会・高松市教育委員会・香川県文化財保護協会）{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）8月8日、「高松市茶臼山古墳」として香川県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 墳丘長：75メートル（または66メートル） * 後円部 ** 直径：35メートル ** 高さ：2.4メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：26メートル ** 高さ：1.8メートル File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|左に1号石室、右奥に2号石室。}}]] 埋葬施設としては後円部墳頂において2基の[[竪穴式石室]]が構築されている。いずれも石室主軸を東西方向とする。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 1号石室（第1主体部：南石室） *: 後円部墳頂南側、墳丘を掘り込んだ墓壙に川原石・粘土を敷いたのち、石室が構築される{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墓壙は東西（長軸）10.7メートル・南北（短軸）6.2メートルを、石室は長さ5.4メートル・最大幅1.1メートル・高さ1.35メートルを測る{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。石室の石材は[[安山岩]]の板石で、小口積みされる{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。天井石の上面は粘土で被覆される。発掘調査では、石室内から人骨2体のほか鍬形石などの副葬品（後述）が検出されている。 * 2号石室（第2主体部：北石室） *: 石室は長さ5.9メートル・最大幅0.8メートル・高さ0.75メートルを測る{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}。石室の石材は安山岩{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または[[花崗岩]]{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}）の小礫である。天井石の上面は粘土で被覆される。発掘調査では石室内から副葬品（後述）が検出されている。 多くの点で畿内色の強い高松茶臼山古墳であるが、石室主軸を東西方向とする点では在地色を示す。 そのほか、前方部で組合式箱形石棺2基、前方部端部裾で組合式箱形石棺2基・土壙墓2基が認められている{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|1号石室内部 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|2号石室内部 == 出土品 == 発掘調査で石室内から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 1号石室 ** 画文帯同行式神獣鏡 1 - 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。 ** 碧玉製鍬形石 2 - 人骨2体の間。朱が塗られる。 ** 管玉 ** 勾玉 ** ガラス製小玉 ** 大刀 1 ** 鉄剣 5 ** 鉄鏃 1 ** [[土師器]]壺 2 * 2号石室 ** 鉄鏃 2 ** 鏃形大型鉄器 2 ** 鉄剣 1 ** 鉄斧 1 ** 鉇 1 ** 小玉 2 鍬形石の出土は、香川県内では唯一の例になる。 なお、前方部の埋葬施設からは副葬品は検出されていない{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 == 文化財 == === 香川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 高松市茶臼山古墳 - 1970年（昭和45年）8月8日指定[http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html 高松市茶臼山古墳]（高松市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（高松市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 38 香川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=三宅良明|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=|title=高松市の文化財 -まもるまなぶつたえる-|publisher=高松市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=香川県埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=2014|chapter=|title=高松市茶臼山古墳|publisher=香川県教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[久本古墳|久本古墳・山下古墳]] - 高松茶臼山古墳の北に所在する後期古墳。久本古墳は高松市指定史跡。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html 高松市茶臼山古墳] - 高松市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:たかまつちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:香川県の古墳]] [[Category:高松市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:香川県指定史跡]]

多くの点で畿内色の強い高松茶臼山古墳であるが、石室主軸を東西方向とする点では在地色を示す。 そのほか、前方部で組合式箱形石棺2基、前方部端部裾で組合式箱形石棺2基・土壙墓2基が認められている{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|1号石室内部 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|2号石室内部 == 出土品 == 発掘調査で石室内から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 1号石室 ** 画文帯同行式神獣鏡 1 - 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。 ** 碧玉製鍬形石 2 - 人骨2体の間。朱が塗られる。 ** 管玉 ** 勾玉 ** ガラス製小玉 ** 大刀 1 ** 鉄剣 5 ** 鉄鏃 1 ** [[土師器]]壺 2 * 2号石室 ** 鉄鏃 2 ** 鏃形大型鉄器 2 ** 鉄剣 1 ** 鉄斧 1 ** 鉇 1 ** 小玉 2 鍬形石の出土は、香川県内では唯一の例になる。 なお、前方部の埋葬施設からは副葬品は検出されていない{{Sfn|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 == 文化財 == === 香川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 高松市茶臼山古墳 - 1970年（昭和45年）8月8日指定[http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html 高松市茶臼山古墳]（高松市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（高松市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 38 香川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=三宅良明|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=|title=高松市の文化財 -まもるまなぶつたえる-|publisher=高松市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=香川県埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=2014|chapter=|title=高松市茶臼山古墳|publisher=香川県教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[久本古墳|久本古墳・山下古墳]] - 高松茶臼山古墳の北に所在する後期古墳。久本古墳は高松市指定史跡。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html 高松市茶臼山古墳] - 高松市ホームページ Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 18|coordinates = 30.10 |coordinates = 134|coordinates = 6|coordinates = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic Site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic Site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高松茶臼山古墳|version=104653395|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:akamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]

File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高松茶臼山古墳|version=104653395|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:akamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu|qq=}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 18|coordinates = 30.10 |coordinates = 134|coordinates = 6|coordinates = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic Site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu|qq=}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai region|qq=}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinju-kyo|qq=}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisamoto Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashita Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高松茶臼山古墳|version=104653395|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:akamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu|qq=}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 18|coordinates = 30.10 |coordinates = 134|coordinates = 6|coordinates = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic Site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu|qq=}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai region|qq=}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|ja|神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinju-kyo|qq=}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisamoto Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashita Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高松茶臼山古墳|version=104653395|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:akamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]

The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]], and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ''' Diameter: 35 meters ''' Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ''' Width: 26 meters ''' Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Animal-motif bronze mirror]] (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisamoto Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashita Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高松茶臼山古墳|version=104653395|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:akamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]

Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]] Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]], and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Animal-motif bronze mirror]] (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic Site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]] Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]], and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Animal-motif bronze mirror]] (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisamoto Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashita Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高松茶臼山古墳|version=104653395|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:akamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:香川県の古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]] Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 18|coordinates = 30.10 |coordinates = 134|coordinates = 6|coordinates = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic Site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]] Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in [[1969]] (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]], and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Animal-motif bronze mirror]] (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisamoto Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashita Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高松茶臼山古墳|version=104653395|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:akamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Kagawa prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:高松市の歴史]]

Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]], and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in [[1970]] (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * [[1969]] (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1970]] (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Animal-motif bronze mirror]] (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisamoto Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashita Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun - Takamatsu City Homepage] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高松茶臼山古墳|version=104653395|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:高松茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:akamatsu Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Kagawa prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamatsu�]] {{Commons category|Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun}}
