Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name = |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu|1=Takamatsu}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, Higashiyamasaki-machi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 18|coordinates = 30.10 |coordinates = 134|coordinates = 6|coordinates = 15.52 |omitted = JP-37 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} |map_size = Mound length: 75m (or 66m)
Height: 2.4m (rear circular section) |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (2) |excavations = Numerous grave goods including magatama stones and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Accompanying tombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} |Deceased = |Imperial tomb = |Historic Site = Designated historic site of Kagawa Prefecture: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Kagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Northeast) |map_dot_label = Takamatsu Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{Coordinate list}} '''Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun''' (高松茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu|1=Takamatsu}} Maeda Nishimachi, Nitta-machi, and Higashiyamasaki-machi. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture (official name: "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun"). == Overview == Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun is situated on the Higashi Chausuyama hill extension of the Kume Mountain range in the eastern part of the Takamatsu Plain, central Kagawa Prefecture{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. An archaeological excavation was conducted in {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969|1=1969}} (Showa 44){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun has a keyhole-shaped structure with the front section oriented northeast. Its mound was built by shaping the natural terrain and piling soil{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The total mound length measures 75 meters{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} (or 66 metersHistoric Site intypeation board.). The exterior of the mound features {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (covering stones) and possibly haniwa (pottery figurines){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of two pit-style stone chambers at the top of the rear circular section, both aligned along the east-west axis. Excavations revealed various grave goods, including magatama stones{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. With its keyhole type, long pit-style stone chambers, use of split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins (presumed), and grave goods such as magatama stones, it exhibits strong characteristics of the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|WD=Q906708|lt=Kinai region|1=Kinai}}. However, its relationship with the local Takamatsu Plain stone mound tombs, including the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|en|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11586812|lt=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}, and its connection to the Yamato Kingdom, offer valuable insights into the political situation of the time{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site of Kagawa Prefecture in {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}} (Showa 45). == Historical Background == * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969|1=1969}} (Showa 44): Excavation by Kagawa Prefecture Education Committee, Takamatsu City Education Committee, and Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site Protection Association{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}} (Showa 45), August 8: Designated as the historic site "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun". == Mound == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: 75 meters (or 66 meters) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 35 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 26 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section == burial == [[File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}{{small|Left: Stone Chamber 1; Right: Stone Chamber 2}}]] The rear circular section contains two pit-style stone chambers aligned along the east-west axis. Details of the chambers are as follows: * '''Stone Chamber 1 (Primary Burial: Southern Chamber):''' Excavated into the southern side of the rear circular section, constructed by laying river stones and clay in the burial pit before building the chamber{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. area: burial pit (long axis: 10.7m, short axis: 6.2m); chamber (length: 5.4m, max width: 1.1m, height: 1.35m). Constructed using andesite slabs{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, covered with clay on the ceiling. * '''Stone Chamber 2 (Secondary Burial: Northern Chamber):''' area: length 5.9m, max width 0.8m, height 0.75m{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}. Constructed using andesite or granite pebbles{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 1 File:Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|Interior of Stone Chamber 2 == Excavated Items == Excavated grave goods include{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * '''Stone Chamber 1''': ** {{ill|Shinju-kyo|en|Shinju-kyo|WD=Q7497614|lt=Animal-motif bronze mirror|1=Shinju-kyo}} (Chinese-imported) ** Two magatama stones (with red pigment) ** Beads: pipe-shaped, comma-shaped, and small glass beads ** Weapons: one sword, five iron swords, one spearhead, two jars * '''Stone Chamber 2''': ** Two iron spearheads ** One axe ** Beads: small glass (2) == Historic Site == === Kagawa Prefecture Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': "Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun" (Historic Site1 August 8, 1970). == notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board by Takamatsu City Education Committee * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 38 - Kagawa Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1989|isbn=4582490387|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|ja|久本古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisamoto Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Hisamoto Kofun|lt=Yamashita Kofun|ja|山下古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashita Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久本古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/usuyamakofun.html Takamatsu Chausuyama Kofun -
